instruction: How do I build an async HTTP server with Boost.Beast that handles multiple routes, serves JSON responses, and manages connections properly?
output:
<think>
Boost.Beast is a C++ HTTP/WebSocket library built on Boost.Asio. Building a server requires understanding several layers: 1) Asio's async I/O model (io_context, strand, completion handlers), 2) Beast's HTTP message types (request, response, string_body), 3) Connection lifecycle (accept, read, process, write, close), 4) Session management via shared_ptr to prevent premature destruction. The architecture: a Listener accepts connections and spawns Sessions. Each Session owns a tcp_stream and handles the HTTP request/response cycle. For routing, we use a simple function dispatch map. Key design points: use `beast::tcp_stream` (not raw `tcp::socket`) for timeout support, use `shared_from_this()` to extend session lifetime through async operations, and use strands for thread safety when running io_context on multiple threads. For JSON responses, we use nlohmann/json or manual string building. Beast requires careful buffer management — the flat_buffer persists across reads for HTTP pipelining.
</think>

Here's a production async HTTP server with Boost.Beast:

```cpp
#include <boost/beast/core.hpp>
#include <boost/beast/http.hpp>
#include <boost/asio.hpp>
#include <memory>
#include <string>
#include <unordered_map>
#include <functional>
#include <iostream>

namespace beast = boost::beast;
namespace http = beast::http;
namespace net = boost::asio;
using tcp = net::ip::tcp;

// === JSON helper (minimal, no dependency) ===
std::string json_object(std::initializer_list<std::pair<std::string, std::string>> fields) {
    std::string result = "{";
    bool first = true;
    for (const auto& [k, v] : fields) {
        if (!first) result += ",";
        result += "\"" + k + "\":\"" + v + "\"";
        first = false;
    }
    return result + "}";
}

// === Router ===
using Request = http::request<http::string_body>;
using Response = http::response<http::string_body>;
using RouteHandler = std::function<Response(const Request&)>;

class Router {
    std::unordered_map<std::string, RouteHandler> routes_;
public:
    void add(const std::string& path, RouteHandler handler) {
        routes_[path] = std::move(handler);
    }

    Response handle(const Request& req) const {
        auto it = routes_.find(std::string(req.target()));
        if (it != routes_.end()) {
            return it->second(req);
        }
        Response res{http::status::not_found, req.version()};
        res.set(http::field::content_type, "application/json");
        res.body() = json_object({{"error", "Not found"}});
        res.prepare_payload();
        return res;
    }
};

// === HTTP Session ===
class Session : public std::enable_shared_from_this<Session> {
    beast::tcp_stream stream_;
    beast::flat_buffer buffer_;
    Request req_;
    std::shared_ptr<Response> res_;
    const Router& router_;

public:
    Session(tcp::socket&& socket, const Router& router)
        : stream_(std::move(socket)), router_(router) {
        stream_.expires_after(std::chrono::seconds(30));
    }

    void run() { do_read(); }

private:
    void do_read() {
        req_ = {};  // Clear previous request
        http::async_read(stream_, buffer_, req_,
            beast::bind_front_handler(&Session::on_read, shared_from_this()));
    }

    void on_read(beast::error_code ec, std::size_t /*bytes*/) {
        if (ec == http::error::end_of_stream) {
            return do_close();
        }
        if (ec) {
            std::cerr << "Read error: " << ec.message() << "\n";
            return;
        }

        // Route and respond
        res_ = std::make_shared<Response>(router_.handle(req_));
        res_->set(http::field::server, "Beast/1.0");
        res_->keep_alive(req_.keep_alive());

        http::async_write(stream_, *res_,
            beast::bind_front_handler(&Session::on_write, shared_from_this()));
    }

    void on_write(beast::error_code ec, std::size_t /*bytes*/) {
        if (ec) {
            std::cerr << "Write error: " << ec.message() << "\n";
            return;
        }
        if (res_->need_eof()) {
            return do_close();
        }
        do_read();  // Keep-alive: read next request
    }

    void do_close() {
        beast::error_code ec;
        stream_.socket().shutdown(tcp::socket::shutdown_send, ec);
    }
};

// === Listener ===
class Listener : public std::enable_shared_from_this<Listener> {
    net::io_context& ioc_;
    tcp::acceptor acceptor_;
    const Router& router_;

public:
    Listener(net::io_context& ioc, tcp::endpoint endpoint, const Router& router)
        : ioc_(ioc), acceptor_(net::make_strand(ioc)), router_(router) {
        acceptor_.open(endpoint.protocol());
        acceptor_.set_option(net::socket_base::reuse_address(true));
        acceptor_.bind(endpoint);
        acceptor_.listen(net::socket_base::max_listen_connections);
    }

    void run() { do_accept(); }

private:
    void do_accept() {
        acceptor_.async_accept(
            net::make_strand(ioc_),
            beast::bind_front_handler(&Listener::on_accept, shared_from_this()));
    }

    void on_accept(beast::error_code ec, tcp::socket socket) {
        if (!ec) {
            std::make_shared<Session>(std::move(socket), router_)->run();
        }
        do_accept();  // Accept next connection
    }
};

int main() {
    Router router;

    router.add("/health", [](const Request& req) -> Response {
        Response res{http::status::ok, req.version()};
        res.set(http::field::content_type, "application/json");
        res.body() = json_object({{"status", "healthy"}});
        res.prepare_payload();
        return res;
    });

    router.add("/api/users", [](const Request& req) -> Response {
        Response res{http::status::ok, req.version()};
        res.set(http::field::content_type, "application/json");
        res.body() = "[{\"id\":1,\"name\":\"Alice\"}]";
        res.prepare_payload();
        return res;
    });

    net::io_context ioc{4};
    auto endpoint = tcp::endpoint{net::ip::make_address("0.0.0.0"), 8080};
    std::make_shared<Listener>(ioc, endpoint, router)->run();

    std::cout << "Listening on :8080\n";

    // Run on multiple threads
    std::vector<std::thread> threads;
    for (int i = 0; i < 3; ++i)
        threads.emplace_back([&ioc] { ioc.run(); });
    ioc.run();
    for (auto& t : threads) t.join();
}
```

instruction: How do I implement a WebSocket server with Boost.Beast that handles multiple clients, supports ping/pong, and provides clean connection management?
output:
<think>
Boost.Beast WebSocket requires understanding the WebSocket lifecycle: 1) HTTP upgrade handshake, 2) Message read/write loop, 3) Close handshake. Key considerations: the WebSocket stream wraps a tcp_stream, supports text and binary messages, and has built-in ping/pong support. For multiple clients, we need a session manager that tracks active connections for broadcasting. Each session runs its own async read loop. For writing, we need a write queue because Beast WebSocket doesn't support concurrent writes — you must wait for one async_write to complete before starting another. The ping/pong mechanism: Beast auto-responds to pings by default (`auto_ping`), but for detecting dead connections, we should send pings periodically and track pong responses. For clean shutdown: send a close frame, wait for the close response, then destroy the session. The session manager should use `std::weak_ptr` to avoid preventing session destruction. Important: WebSocket reads and writes on the same stream are NOT thread-safe — use a strand.
</think>

Here's a production WebSocket server with Boost.Beast:

```cpp
#include <boost/beast/core.hpp>
#include <boost/beast/websocket.hpp>
#include <boost/asio.hpp>
#include <memory>
#include <set>
#include <queue>
#include <mutex>
#include <iostream>

namespace beast = boost::beast;
namespace websocket = beast::websocket;
namespace net = boost::asio;
using tcp = net::ip::tcp;

// === Connection manager for broadcasting ===
class WsSession;

class ConnectionManager {
    mutable std::mutex mutex_;
    std::set<std::shared_ptr<WsSession>> sessions_;
public:
    void join(std::shared_ptr<WsSession> session) {
        std::lock_guard lock(mutex_);
        sessions_.insert(session);
        std::cout << "Client connected. Total: " << sessions_.size() << "\n";
    }

    void leave(std::shared_ptr<WsSession> session) {
        std::lock_guard lock(mutex_);
        sessions_.erase(session);
        std::cout << "Client disconnected. Total: " << sessions_.size() << "\n";
    }

    void broadcast(const std::string& message);
    size_t count() const {
        std::lock_guard lock(mutex_);
        return sessions_.size();
    }
};

// === WebSocket session ===
class WsSession : public std::enable_shared_from_this<WsSession> {
    websocket::stream<beast::tcp_stream> ws_;
    beast::flat_buffer buffer_;
    ConnectionManager& manager_;
    std::queue<std::string> write_queue_;
    bool writing_ = false;
    net::steady_timer ping_timer_;
    bool pong_received_ = true;

public:
    WsSession(tcp::socket&& socket, ConnectionManager& manager)
        : ws_(std::move(socket))
        , manager_(manager)
        , ping_timer_(ws_.get_executor()) {

        ws_.set_option(websocket::stream_base::timeout::suggested(beast::role_type::server));
        ws_.set_option(websocket::stream_base::decorator(
            [](websocket::response_type& res) {
                res.set(boost::beast::http::field::server, "Beast-WS/1.0");
            }));

        // Track pong responses
        ws_.control_callback(
            [this](websocket::frame_type kind, beast::string_view /*payload*/) {
                if (kind == websocket::frame_type::pong) {
                    pong_received_ = true;
                }
            });
    }

    void run() {
        ws_.async_accept(
            beast::bind_front_handler(&WsSession::on_accept, shared_from_this()));
    }

    void send(const std::string& msg) {
        net::post(ws_.get_executor(),
            beast::bind_front_handler(&WsSession::do_send, shared_from_this(), msg));
    }

private:
    void on_accept(beast::error_code ec) {
        if (ec) return;
        manager_.join(shared_from_this());
        start_ping_timer();
        do_read();
    }

    void do_read() {
        ws_.async_read(buffer_,
            beast::bind_front_handler(&WsSession::on_read, shared_from_this()));
    }

    void on_read(beast::error_code ec, std::size_t bytes_transferred) {
        if (ec) {
            if (ec != websocket::error::closed)
                std::cerr << "Read error: " << ec.message() << "\n";
            return cleanup();
        }

        std::string message = beast::buffers_to_string(buffer_.data());
        buffer_.consume(bytes_transferred);

        // Echo back + broadcast
        std::string response = "{\"echo\":\"" + message + "\"}";
        send(response);

        do_read();
    }

    void do_send(const std::string& msg) {
        write_queue_.push(msg);
        if (!writing_) {
            do_write();
        }
    }

    void do_write() {
        if (write_queue_.empty()) {
            writing_ = false;
            return;
        }
        writing_ = true;
        auto msg = std::make_shared<std::string>(write_queue_.front());
        write_queue_.pop();

        ws_.text(true);
        ws_.async_write(net::buffer(*msg),
            [self = shared_from_this(), msg](beast::error_code ec, std::size_t) {
                if (ec) return self->cleanup();
                self->do_write();  // Process next in queue
            });
    }

    void start_ping_timer() {
        ping_timer_.expires_after(std::chrono::seconds(30));
        ping_timer_.async_wait([self = shared_from_this()](beast::error_code ec) {
            if (ec) return;
            if (!self->pong_received_) {
                // Client didn't respond to last ping — dead connection
                self->ws_.next_layer().close();
                return;
            }
            self->pong_received_ = false;
            self->ws_.async_ping({},
                [self](beast::error_code ec) {
                    if (!ec) self->start_ping_timer();
                });
        });
    }

    void cleanup() {
        ping_timer_.cancel();
        manager_.leave(shared_from_this());
    }
};

void ConnectionManager::broadcast(const std::string& message) {
    std::lock_guard lock(mutex_);
    for (auto& session : sessions_) {
        session->send(message);
    }
}

// === Listener ===
class WsListener : public std::enable_shared_from_this<WsListener> {
    net::io_context& ioc_;
    tcp::acceptor acceptor_;
    ConnectionManager& manager_;
public:
    WsListener(net::io_context& ioc, tcp::endpoint ep, ConnectionManager& mgr)
        : ioc_(ioc), acceptor_(ioc), manager_(mgr) {
        acceptor_.open(ep.protocol());
        acceptor_.set_option(net::socket_base::reuse_address(true));
        acceptor_.bind(ep);
        acceptor_.listen();
    }

    void run() { do_accept(); }
private:
    void do_accept() {
        acceptor_.async_accept(net::make_strand(ioc_),
            [self = shared_from_this()](beast::error_code ec, tcp::socket socket) {
                if (!ec)
                    std::make_shared<WsSession>(std::move(socket), self->manager_)->run();
                self->do_accept();
            });
    }
};

int main() {
    net::io_context ioc{2};
    ConnectionManager manager;
    auto ep = tcp::endpoint{net::ip::make_address("0.0.0.0"), 9001};
    std::make_shared<WsListener>(ioc, ep, manager)->run();
    std::cout << "WebSocket server on :9001\n";
    ioc.run();
}
```

instruction: How do I add SSL/TLS support to a Boost.Beast HTTP server with proper certificate handling, session resumption, and ALPN?
output:
<think>
Adding SSL to Beast requires Boost.Asio's SSL support (`boost::asio::ssl`). The key components: 1) `ssl::context` configured with certificate, private key, and protocol options, 2) `beast::ssl_stream<beast::tcp_stream>` which wraps the TCP stream with SSL, 3) The SSL handshake must complete before any HTTP operations, 4) For production: session caching for TLS resumption, ALPN for HTTP/2 negotiation, proper cipher selection, OCSP stapling. The architecture: Listener accepts TCP connections, creates ssl_stream, performs handshake, then passes to Session. The ssl::context should be shared across all sessions (it's thread-safe after initialization). Important settings: disable SSLv2/v3 and TLS 1.0/1.1, set modern cipher suites, enable session cache for performance. For ALPN (Application-Layer Protocol Negotiation), we set the callback to advertise supported protocols. Session resumption via TLS session tickets reduces handshake latency for returning clients. I should also show how to handle both HTTP and HTTPS on different ports, and the SNI (Server Name Indication) callback for virtual hosting.
</think>

Here's an SSL-enabled Boost.Beast server:

```cpp
#include <boost/beast/core.hpp>
#include <boost/beast/http.hpp>
#include <boost/beast/ssl.hpp>
#include <boost/asio.hpp>
#include <boost/asio/ssl.hpp>
#include <memory>
#include <iostream>

namespace beast = boost::beast;
namespace http = beast::http;
namespace net = boost::asio;
namespace ssl = net::ssl;
using tcp = net::ip::tcp;

// === SSL context configuration ===
ssl::context create_ssl_context(
    const std::string& cert_file,
    const std::string& key_file,
    const std::string& dh_file = "")
{
    ssl::context ctx{ssl::context::tlsv12};

    // Security: only TLS 1.2+ with modern ciphers
    ctx.set_options(
        ssl::context::default_workarounds |
        ssl::context::no_sslv2 |
        ssl::context::no_sslv3 |
        ssl::context::no_tlsv1 |
        ssl::context::no_tlsv1_1 |
        ssl::context::single_dh_use);

    // Set cipher preference (server chooses)
    SSL_CTX_set_options(ctx.native_handle(), SSL_OP_CIPHER_SERVER_PREFERENCE);
    SSL_CTX_set_cipher_list(ctx.native_handle(),
        "ECDHE-ECDSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384:"
        "ECDHE-RSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384:"
        "ECDHE-ECDSA-CHACHA20-POLY1305:"
        "ECDHE-RSA-CHACHA20-POLY1305:"
        "ECDHE-ECDSA-AES128-GCM-SHA256:"
        "ECDHE-RSA-AES128-GCM-SHA256");

    // Load certificate chain and private key
    ctx.use_certificate_chain_file(cert_file);
    ctx.use_private_key_file(key_file, ssl::context::pem);

    // DH parameters for forward secrecy
    if (!dh_file.empty()) {
        ctx.use_tmp_dh_file(dh_file);
    }

    // Enable TLS session resumption (session cache)
    SSL_CTX_set_session_cache_mode(ctx.native_handle(),
        SSL_SESS_CACHE_SERVER | SSL_SESS_CACHE_NO_INTERNAL);
    SSL_CTX_sess_set_cache_size(ctx.native_handle(), 1024);

    // ALPN: advertise HTTP/1.1
    SSL_CTX_set_alpn_select_cb(ctx.native_handle(),
        [](SSL*, const unsigned char** out, unsigned char* outlen,
           const unsigned char* in, unsigned int inlen, void*) -> int {
            // Select http/1.1
            static const unsigned char alpn[] = {8, 'h', 't', 't', 'p', '/', '1', '.', '1'};
            if (SSL_select_next_proto(
                    const_cast<unsigned char**>(out), outlen,
                    alpn, sizeof(alpn), in, inlen) == OPENSSL_NPN_NEGOTIATED) {
                return SSL_TLSEXT_ERR_OK;
            }
            return SSL_TLSEXT_ERR_NOACK;
        }, nullptr);

    return ctx;
}

// === SSL Session ===
class SslSession : public std::enable_shared_from_this<SslSession> {
    beast::ssl_stream<beast::tcp_stream> stream_;
    beast::flat_buffer buffer_;
    http::request<http::string_body> req_;

public:
    SslSession(tcp::socket&& socket, ssl::context& ctx)
        : stream_(std::move(socket), ctx) {
        beast::get_lowest_layer(stream_).expires_after(std::chrono::seconds(30));
    }

    void run() {
        // Perform SSL handshake first
        stream_.async_handshake(ssl::stream_base::server,
            beast::bind_front_handler(&SslSession::on_handshake, shared_from_this()));
    }

private:
    void on_handshake(beast::error_code ec) {
        if (ec) {
            std::cerr << "SSL handshake error: " << ec.message() << "\n";
            return;
        }
        do_read();
    }

    void do_read() {
        req_ = {};
        beast::get_lowest_layer(stream_).expires_after(std::chrono::seconds(30));
        http::async_read(stream_, buffer_, req_,
            beast::bind_front_handler(&SslSession::on_read, shared_from_this()));
    }

    void on_read(beast::error_code ec, std::size_t) {
        if (ec == http::error::end_of_stream) return do_close();
        if (ec) return;

        auto res = std::make_shared<http::response<http::string_body>>(
            http::status::ok, req_.version());
        res->set(http::field::server, "Beast-SSL/1.0");
        res->set(http::field::content_type, "application/json");
        res->set(http::field::strict_transport_security, "max-age=63072000");
        res->body() = "{\"secure\":true,\"protocol\":\"TLS\"}";
        res->prepare_payload();
        res->keep_alive(req_.keep_alive());

        http::async_write(stream_, *res,
            [self = shared_from_this(), res](beast::error_code ec, std::size_t) {
                if (ec) return;
                if (res->need_eof()) self->do_close();
                else self->do_read();
            });
    }

    void do_close() {
        beast::get_lowest_layer(stream_).expires_after(std::chrono::seconds(5));
        stream_.async_shutdown(
            [self = shared_from_this()](beast::error_code) {});
    }
};

// === SSL Listener ===
class SslListener : public std::enable_shared_from_this<SslListener> {
    net::io_context& ioc_;
    ssl::context& ctx_;
    tcp::acceptor acceptor_;

public:
    SslListener(net::io_context& ioc, ssl::context& ctx, tcp::endpoint ep)
        : ioc_(ioc), ctx_(ctx), acceptor_(net::make_strand(ioc)) {
        acceptor_.open(ep.protocol());
        acceptor_.set_option(net::socket_base::reuse_address(true));
        acceptor_.bind(ep);
        acceptor_.listen();
    }

    void run() { do_accept(); }

private:
    void do_accept() {
        acceptor_.async_accept(net::make_strand(ioc_),
            [self = shared_from_this()](beast::error_code ec, tcp::socket socket) {
                if (!ec)
                    std::make_shared<SslSession>(std::move(socket), self->ctx_)->run();
                self->do_accept();
            });
    }
};

int main() {
    auto ctx = create_ssl_context("server.crt", "server.key", "dh2048.pem");
    net::io_context ioc{4};
    auto ep = tcp::endpoint{net::ip::make_address("0.0.0.0"), 8443};
    std::make_shared<SslListener>(ioc, ctx, ep)->run();
    std::cout << "HTTPS server on :8443\n";

    std::vector<std::thread> threads;
    for (int i = 0; i < 3; ++i)
        threads.emplace_back([&ioc] { ioc.run(); });
    ioc.run();
    for (auto& t : threads) t.join();
}
```

instruction: How do I implement connection pooling and management for a Boost.Beast HTTP client with retry logic, timeouts, and keep-alive support?
output:
<think>
A production HTTP client needs more than one-shot requests. Key requirements: 1) Connection pooling — reuse TCP connections to the same host (HTTP keep-alive), 2) Connection limits per host to prevent resource exhaustion, 3) Timeout management at both connection and request level, 4) Retry logic with exponential backoff for transient failures, 5) Health monitoring — detect and remove dead connections. The pool design: map from host:port to a queue of idle connections. When a request needs a connection, check the pool first. If none available and under limit, create new. If at limit, wait. After response, return connection to pool if keep-alive. Idle connections need a TTL — periodically close connections that have been idle too long. For Beast specifically: `beast::tcp_stream` has `expires_after` for timeouts, and we check `res.keep_alive()` to decide whether to return to pool. For retry: only retry on connection errors and 5xx responses, not 4xx. Use exponential backoff with jitter. The pool should be thread-safe via strand or mutex.
</think>

Here's a connection-pooling HTTP client with Boost.Beast:

```cpp
#include <boost/beast/core.hpp>
#include <boost/beast/http.hpp>
#include <boost/asio.hpp>
#include <memory>
#include <queue>
#include <unordered_map>
#include <mutex>
#include <chrono>
#include <random>
#include <iostream>

namespace beast = boost::beast;
namespace http = beast::http;
namespace net = boost::asio;
using tcp = net::ip::tcp;

using Clock = std::chrono::steady_clock;

// === Pooled connection ===
struct PooledConnection {
    beast::tcp_stream stream;
    Clock::time_point last_used;
    bool in_use = false;

    PooledConnection(net::any_io_executor ex)
        : stream(ex), last_used(Clock::now()) {}
};

// === Connection pool ===
class ConnectionPool {
public:
    struct Config {
        size_t max_connections_per_host = 10;
        std::chrono::seconds idle_timeout{60};
        std::chrono::seconds connect_timeout{5};
        std::chrono::seconds request_timeout{30};
        int max_retries = 3;
        std::chrono::milliseconds base_backoff{100};
    };

private:
    net::io_context& ioc_;
    Config config_;
    tcp::resolver resolver_;

    struct HostPool {
        std::queue<std::shared_ptr<PooledConnection>> idle;
        size_t active_count = 0;
    };

    std::mutex mutex_;
    std::unordered_map<std::string, HostPool> pools_;
    net::steady_timer cleanup_timer_;

    std::string make_key(const std::string& host, uint16_t port) {
        return host + ":" + std::to_string(port);
    }

public:
    ConnectionPool(net::io_context& ioc, Config config = {})
        : ioc_(ioc), config_(config)
        , resolver_(net::make_strand(ioc))
        , cleanup_timer_(ioc) {
        schedule_cleanup();
    }

    // Synchronous request with retry
    http::response<http::string_body> request(
        http::verb method,
        const std::string& host,
        uint16_t port,
        const std::string& target,
        const std::string& body = "")
    {
        beast::error_code ec;
        std::random_device rd;
        std::mt19937 rng(rd());

        for (int attempt = 0; attempt <= config_.max_retries; ++attempt) {
            if (attempt > 0) {
                // Exponential backoff with jitter
                auto delay = config_.base_backoff * (1 << (attempt - 1));
                auto jitter = std::chrono::milliseconds(
                    std::uniform_int_distribution<int>(0, delay.count() / 2)(rng));
                std::this_thread::sleep_for(delay + jitter);
                std::cout << "Retry " << attempt << " for " << host << target << "\n";
            }

            auto conn = acquire(host, port, ec);
            if (ec) continue;

            try {
                // Build request
                http::request<http::string_body> req{method, target, 11};
                req.set(http::field::host, host);
                req.set(http::field::user_agent, "Beast-Pool/1.0");
                req.set(http::field::connection, "keep-alive");
                if (!body.empty()) {
                    req.set(http::field::content_type, "application/json");
                    req.body() = body;
                    req.prepare_payload();
                }

                // Send
                conn->stream.expires_after(config_.request_timeout);
                http::write(conn->stream, req, ec);
                if (ec) { discard(conn, host, port); continue; }

                // Receive
                beast::flat_buffer buffer;
                http::response<http::string_body> res;
                http::read(conn->stream, buffer, res, ec);
                if (ec) { discard(conn, host, port); continue; }

                // Return connection to pool if keep-alive
                if (res.keep_alive()) {
                    release(conn, host, port);
                } else {
                    discard(conn, host, port);
                }

                // Don't retry client errors
                if (res.result_int() >= 500 && attempt < config_.max_retries) {
                    continue;
                }

                return res;
            } catch (...) {
                discard(conn, host, port);
                if (attempt == config_.max_retries) throw;
            }
        }

        throw beast::system_error(ec);
    }

private:
    std::shared_ptr<PooledConnection> acquire(
        const std::string& host, uint16_t port, beast::error_code& ec)
    {
        auto key = make_key(host, port);
        std::lock_guard lock(mutex_);
        auto& pool = pools_[key];

        // Try to get an idle connection
        while (!pool.idle.empty()) {
            auto conn = pool.idle.front();
            pool.idle.pop();

            // Check if still alive (not idle too long)
            auto idle_time = Clock::now() - conn->last_used;
            if (idle_time < config_.idle_timeout) {
                conn->in_use = true;
                return conn;
            }
            pool.active_count--;
        }

        // Create new connection if under limit
        if (pool.active_count >= config_.max_connections_per_host) {
            ec = net::error::no_descriptors;
            return nullptr;
        }

        auto conn = std::make_shared<PooledConnection>(ioc_.get_executor());
        conn->stream.expires_after(config_.connect_timeout);

        auto results = resolver_.resolve(host, std::to_string(port), ec);
        if (ec) return nullptr;

        conn->stream.connect(results, ec);
        if (ec) return nullptr;

        conn->in_use = true;
        pool.active_count++;
        return conn;
    }

    void release(std::shared_ptr<PooledConnection> conn,
                 const std::string& host, uint16_t port) {
        std::lock_guard lock(mutex_);
        conn->in_use = false;
        conn->last_used = Clock::now();
        pools_[make_key(host, port)].idle.push(conn);
    }

    void discard(std::shared_ptr<PooledConnection> conn,
                 const std::string& host, uint16_t port) {
        beast::error_code ec;
        conn->stream.socket().shutdown(tcp::socket::shutdown_both, ec);
        std::lock_guard lock(mutex_);
        pools_[make_key(host, port)].active_count--;
    }

    void schedule_cleanup() {
        cleanup_timer_.expires_after(std::chrono::seconds(30));
        cleanup_timer_.async_wait([this](beast::error_code ec) {
            if (ec) return;
            std::lock_guard lock(mutex_);
            auto now = Clock::now();
            for (auto& [key, pool] : pools_) {
                size_t before = pool.idle.size();
                std::queue<std::shared_ptr<PooledConnection>> fresh;
                while (!pool.idle.empty()) {
                    auto c = pool.idle.front(); pool.idle.pop();
                    if (now - c->last_used < config_.idle_timeout)
                        fresh.push(c);
                    else
                        pool.active_count--;
                }
                pool.idle = std::move(fresh);
            }
            schedule_cleanup();
        });
    }
};

int main() {
    net::io_context ioc;
    ConnectionPool pool(ioc, {
        .max_connections_per_host = 10,
        .idle_timeout = std::chrono::seconds(60),
        .max_retries = 3,
    });

    auto res = pool.request(http::verb::get, "httpbin.org", 80, "/get");
    std::cout << "Status: " << res.result_int() << "\n";
    std::cout << "Body: " << res.body() << "\n";
}
```
